concerns.text: T030a (Bayesian Correlation) is listed as a parallel task [P] alongside T029a/T029c (Data Generation). However, T030a explicitly consumes the output of T029a/T029c ('correlate lifetime with Solvation Energy'). The [P] tag is incorrect; T030a must be sequential to T029a/T029c. This violates the 'Producer before consumer' dependency rule.

T030b (Statistical Reporting) is listed as [P] (parallel) but the task description states it must 'report Posterior Probability... and Bayes Factors' based on the output of T030a. T030b cannot execute before T030a completes. The [P] tag is invalid and creates a hidden ordering violation.
T034 (Generate figures) is listed as [P] but depends on T030b's output ('reading data/processed/correlation_results.json from T030b'). The [P] tag is incorrect; T034 must be sequential to T030b.

T039 (Integration Test) is listed as [P] but the description explicitly states it 'depends on T026, T034, AND completion of Phase 4 and Phase 5'. The [P] tag is incorrect; T039 is a sequential gate task that cannot run in parallel with the upstream phases it validates.

T029d and T029b are listed as [P] (parallel) but T029d depends on T029a and T029b depends on T029c. While T029a/T029c can run in parallel, the parsers (T029d/T029b) must wait for their respective generators. The [P] tag on the parsers is misleading as they are not independent of the generation tasks.
